name: Bucatini Alla’amatriciana

ingredients.item: inch round of guanciale cut into small pieces
can San Marzano tomatoes plus one can of water

method: In a large frying pan on medium heat, fry guanciale extra crispy. Remove guanciale and set aside, but keep half grease, leaving the remaining smaller bits of guanciale. Add red pepper flakes to the hot grease and fry a little, then add the wine and let the alcohol burn off. Add tomatoes, water, salt, and pepper and stir well. Let simmer on low for about 20 minutes. In the meantime, prepare the bucatini pasta. Add half the reserved guanciale to sauce and a ladle of pasta water, as much as needed to make a nice sauce. Add pasta directly to the sauce, mix, and manticare. Serving suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Sprinkle grated pecorino cheese on top. Serves 2-3. Olé
